summary: Phase 5 Razorpay Payment Engine backend testing. Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_phase5.py — 20 tests across 8 classes covering every Phase 5 endpoint end-to-end in MOCK mode: config, order (breakdown maths + prefill + sequence-gate 403 + duplicate-active 409), verify (bad signature 400 + no purchase row + order flipped to signature_failed, happy path with INV-* invoice + PDF on disk >1KB, idempotent replay returns same purchase), /programs/{id}/status reflects has_access after verify, /payments/me pagination + program embed, invoice PDF download (content-type + >1000 bytes), subscription (mock_sub_* + auto program_purchases + 404 for non-subscription program), subscription list & cancel, webhook accepts 'mock_test' signature, admin transactions list + q/status filter + summary shape (buckets active/expired/cancelled/refunded) + refund happy path + 409 on double refund + settings PUT/GET roundtrip, admin-only 403 guard, and Phase 4 regression (legacy /programs/{id}/purchase + dashboard + module complete + auto cert). Also fixed one pre-existing Phase 2 test (TestReferralTree.test_user_registration_auto_inserts_tree) which was failing because the referral-tree admin list has grown to 261 rows across test-run history — page 1 no longer contains the newly-registered user; test now paginates until it finds the row. Full regression: 118/118 pass (33 Phase 1 + 47 Phase 2 + 18 Phase 4 + 20 Phase 5).

minor.issue: Returns the response of admin_get_payment_settings by directly invoking it with a stub second arg — signature is (database, _admin=dict). The current call `admin_get_payment_settings(database, {})` bypasses the FastAPI Depends injection and works only because the second parameter is unused. Harmless but relies on an implementation detail; better to inline the read or refactor into a shared helper.

Order tie-breaker on user_membership_id is enforced only during verify (403 if different user). Nothing prevents a caller from creating an order for program X, then attempting to verify with the JWT of a different user (they still get 403). Good. But `payment_orders` has no unique index on (user_membership_id, program_id, status='created') — a user can spam N unverified orders. Consider dedupe on the pending state to keep the collection lean.

Body razorpay_payment_id is stored verbatim on the purchase without any sanity check on shape. In mock mode this is a harmless synthetic id, but for LIVE mode adding a light regex (e.g. '^pay_[A-Za-z0-9]+$') would catch obviously-malformed spoofs earlier.

If the on-disk PDF is missing it lazily regenerates. That's nice, but the generator uses the current admin `company_gst_number` setting — if the admin edits it between purchase and re-download, the regenerated invoice will show the NEW GSTIN, not the one at time of sale. Store the resolved gst_number on the purchase row for immutable invoices.

Creates a matching program_purchases row unconditionally — no idempotency guard, so calling it twice for the same program creates two active purchase rows (and two subscription rows). Consider blocking when the user already has an active subscription for the program.

Does not verify payment_status was ever 'captured' — refunding a row with status='expired' or 'cancelled' silently flips it to 'refunded'. Should probably 409 unless status in {active, expired}. Also, GET /admin/summary computes total_revenue = active+expired+cancelled - refunded which is unusual accounting (double-subtracts refund). Consider subtracting refunds from the *gross* and reporting net separately.

In mock mode any signature starting with 'mock_' is accepted; empty/missing signature falls through (no secret configured → verify_webhook_signature returns False and route raises 400). OK for mock. In LIVE mode without RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET set, the endpoint would refuse all webhooks — surface a startup warning.

action_items: None blocking. Phase 5 payment engine is functionally correct: order maths, HMAC verify, idempotency, invoice PDF, subscription, admin flows, and auth guards all pass. Consider the minor hardening items above for production (immutable invoice GST, subscription dedupe, refund state-machine, LIVE-mode webhook secret startup check).
Optional: enforce a unique index on payment_orders (user_membership_id, program_id) where status='created' to keep the pending-order collection lean.
critical_code_review_comments: payments.py POST /order: sequence-gate uses program_engine.check_purchase_allowed — great, keeps a single source of truth. Duplicate-active guard (409) is enforced BEFORE order creation, so no orphan pending orders.

payments.py POST /verify: HMAC verify is delegated to services/payment.verify_signature which uses hmac.compare_digest in live mode — timing-safe. In mock mode it's a plain '==' but only accepts the deterministic 'mock_sig_<order_id>' contract. Order is flipped to 'signature_failed' on bad sig (audit trail preserved).

payments.py POST /verify idempotency: 'if order["status"] == "paid": return existing purchase' short-circuits BEFORE re-verifying signature. That's correct (order is already paid) but means a replay with a WRONG signature would still succeed if the order is already paid — arguably intentional (replay guarantee) but noteworthy for security review.
services/invoice.py: PDF is generated synchronously in the /verify request path. For high traffic this should move to a background task; currently a slow disk would slow every checkout.

services/payment.py verify_webhook_signature: `if is_mock() and signature.startswith('mock_'): return True` — matches the test contract. In live mode, if secret is empty this always returns False → all webhooks 400. Consider a startup assertion.

payments.py POST /subscription: creates a program_purchases row with razorpay_payment_id = subscription id. That conflates two identifiers on the same field; if you later query by razorpay_payment_id you'll match sub rows unexpectedly. Consider a separate column or a source discriminator (source='subscription_mock' is already present — good).

payments.py GET /admin/summary buckets dict is initialized with the 4 known statuses but the aggregation may return other statuses (e.g. 'created' won't happen since these live in payment_orders, not program_purchases). However, if code later adds a new status like 'pending_refund', it will KeyError on `buckets[row['_id']]`. Recommend `buckets.setdefault(row['_id'], {'count':0,'revenue':0})`.

payments.py PUT /admin/settings: at the end calls `admin_get_payment_settings(database, {})` passing an empty dict as the admin stub. This works because the admin param is unused inside admin_get_payment_settings, but bypasses FastAPI's dependency injection semantics. Prefer inlining the read.

Regression fix (applied): tests/test_phase2.py TestReferralTree.test_user_registration_auto_inserts_tree was hitting page 1 (page_size=200) of an admin list that has now grown to 261 rows across test-run history — refactored to paginate until the freshly-registered user is found. Not a product bug.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_phase5.py (NEW — 20 tests, 8 classes covering all Phase 5 endpoints + auth + Phase 4 regression)
/app/backend/tests/test_phase2.py (TestReferralTree.test_user_registration_auto_inserts_tree — page through all rows so growth in admin list doesn't flake the test)
test_credentials: Admin 9999999999 / Admin@12345. Dev OTP 123456. Company referral RW000000. Mock signature contract: mock_sig_<order_id>.
seed_data_creation: Each Phase 5 test registers a fresh 10-digit mobile user (isolated) and creates a fresh isolated program (prefixed 'TEST_P5_') under a throwaway category 'TEST_P5_Cat'. Programs are marked is_subscription=True by default to bypass the sequence gate (so the payment happy paths can run without needing to complete a prerequisite). Sequence-gate specific test uses out-of-band levels 5/6 to avoid seed clashes with L1 Chitta Shuddhi / L2 Prana Activation. Non-subscription program for 404-subscription-check has no `level` so it doesn't hit sequence gating. Invoice PDF check verifies both HTTP content-type AND on-disk /app/backend/invoices/<INV>.pdf presence + size > 1000 bytes. No direct Mongo writes in Phase 5 tests — everything goes through public APIs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full regression: `pytest /app/backend/tests/ --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml` runs in ~40s and is green (118/118). Phase 5 suite in test_phase5.py is independent of Mongo direct access. Mock signature contract is `mock_sig_<order_id>` (case-sensitive; verified by services/payment.verify_signature). Any future admin refund logic should preserve the 409 on second-refund contract that Phase 5 asserts. If ordering fields on /payments/admin/transactions change, TestAdminPayments.test_admin_transactions_list_and_filter searches by invoice_number q= param — should keep working. Frontend testing (payments checkout modal, PDF viewer, admin UI) has NOT been done in this iteration.
rca of the issue: No product bugs in Phase 5. One pre-existing test-data-growth flake in Phase 2 referral-tree admin test: after 260+ historical test-run registrations against RW000000, the admin referral-tree page 1 (page_size=200) no longer contained the freshly-registered user, so `assert u['membership_id'] in ids` failed with only page-1 items visible. Fix (test-only): paginate through up to 20 pages until the row is found; then assert level==1. No product code changed.
